Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Optimizing Recommendation Systems with JDK's Vector API 31 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. Show HN: Jido 2.0, Elixir Agent Framework 158 upvotes, 35 comments
  3. GPT 5.4 Thinking and Pro 54 upvotes, 9 comments
  4. A GitHub Issue Title Compromised 4k Developer Machines 133 upvotes, 31 comments
  5. Hardware hotplug events on Linux, the gory details 11 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. Google Workspace CLI 852 upvotes, 269 comments
  7. Wikipedia in read-only mode following mass admin account compromise 545 upvotes, 170 comments
  8. Building a new Flash 681 upvotes, 226 comments
  9. Nvidia PersonaPlex 7B on Apple Silicon: Full-Duplex Speech-to-Speech in Swift 325 upvotes, 105 comments
  10. Jails for NetBSD – Kernel Enforced Isolation and Native Resource Control 89 upvotes, 23 comments
  11. Google Safe Browsing missed 84% of confirmed phishing sites 216 upvotes, 67 comments
  12. Poor Man's Polaroid 162 upvotes, 48 comments
  13. GPT-5.4 Thinking and GPT-5.4 Pro 80 upvotes, 21 comments
  14. Datasets for Reconstructing Visual Perception from Brain Data 30 upvotes, 6 comments
  15. The Government Uses Targeted Advertising to Track Your Location 129 upvotes, 48 comments
  16. Fast-Servers 75 upvotes, 25 comments
  17. World-first gigabit laser link between aircraft and geostationary satellite 132 upvotes, 51 comments
  18. US asked Ukraine for help fighting Iranian drones, Zelensky says 44 upvotes, 15 comments
  19. Smalltalk's Browser: Unbeatable, yet Not Enough 119 upvotes, 59 comments
  20. Greg Kroah-Hartman Stretches Support Periods for Key Linux LTS Kernels 32 upvotes, 12 comments
  21. Show HN: PageAgent, A GUI agent that lives inside your web app 41 upvotes, 17 comments
  22. Good software knows when to stop 217 upvotes, 132 comments
  23. The Man Who Broke into Jail 58 upvotes, 33 comments
  24. GPT-5.4 Thinking System Card 154 upvotes, 105 comments
  25. Intelligence is a commodity. Context is the real AI Moat 107 upvotes, 73 comments
  26. The Brand Age 47 upvotes, 27 comments
  27. Let's Get Physical 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  28. AMD will bring its “Ryzen AI” processors to standard desktop PCs for first time 197 upvotes, 181 comments
  29. Relicensing with AI-Assisted Rewrite 331 upvotes, 332 comments
